title: Fonts used in the Emergency overlays in the comm
display_title: Fonts used in the Emergency overlays in the comm
wikitext:
[]: Fonts used in the "Emergency" overlays in the comm             Quote Post by xan » Sun Apr 17, 2011 10:36 pm Hi there, I'm about to start a little project that will include reproductions of some of the warning/emergency signs that appear floating on the command center in Evangelion when an Angel attacks. For this I'd need (or at least I'd like) to know which fonts are used in the series. Especifically: http://dump.robhoward.id.au/pics/misc/CountdownShrunk/CountdownEMRGSimplehex.png.jpg The font for "EMERGENCY". It looks a lot like Helvetica, but you can tell it's not because the mouth of the C is much wider. http://www.imgspark.com/icache/0065/d206fffa4bc8edd65b53c47b440d8f7f_l.jpg The font for the kanjis, and also for the text that appears below (don't have the series handy, so cannot show a full shot...). Also if somebody knows the pattern for the text below, that would be useful. I seem to remember that it had at least something like "Nth Angel Detected", both in English and Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 18, 2011 8:07 am Two things that come to mind that might be useful: Gainax Pages did a nice feature a while back about the typography in Evangelion, which might interest you. http://replay.waybackmachine.org/20081222052937/http://www.gainaxpages.com/features/typography.php Alternatively, I frequently use WhatTheFont to determine what a font is, or what font is a relatively close match. http://new.myfonts.com/WhatTheFont/

[]:             Quote Post by xan » Sun Apr 24, 2011 6:06 pm Hey, thanks for the reply, would have been difficult to figure out the font for the kanjis otherwise. In case you are interested, I already have something working, I put a sneak preview on youtube: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zR67NtGaQqs
